reasoning: Based on the fetched FRED dataset, the April 2026 observation for EXPINF5YR is 2.485, which serves as the comparison threshold. This value sits significantly above the trailing 12-month mean (approx. 2.35) and effectively acts as a local maximum (about 2 standard deviations above the trend line given residual std of 0.067). Due to mean reversion, near-term observations are highly likely to drop below this outlier threshold, justifying a low initial probability of an increase (0.15 for the 1-month horizon). The FRED statistical tool provided a combined P(increase) estimate of 0.26, which I use as a sturdy anchor for the mid-term (1-year) horizon. As the forecast horizon extends to 3, 5, and 10 years, uncertainty grows regarding structural shifts in inflation expectations, moving the probability progressively closer to 0.5.
